paper_title: A Critical Analysis of Red Ceramic Blocks Roughness Estimation by 2D and 3D Methods
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/12_A critical analysis of red ceramic blocks roughness estimation by 2D and 3D methods.pdf

equations_checked.notes: Visually verified as Ra approximately equals (1/n) times the sum of absolute zi. The 3D method later defines zi as Z-direction residuals from one least-squares fitting plane; the paper uses Ra terminology for both profile and areal calculations.

tables_checked.notes: All 48 surfaces and four H/V line columns were visually checked. The body text calls them mean Ra values while the title says standard deviation from 2D Ra data; individual entries were not reused as model parameters.

F, F-critical, p-values and Y/N outcomes were visually checked. The pooled H1H2 versus V1V2 result (2.2731, 1.4038, 0.00004) is retained with the caveat that several individual pairs are not significant.
All 48 paired Avg 3D/Avg 2D values were visually checked; only the aggregate means from Table 4 are retained.
Retained 3D/2D means 2.2701/1.6697, variances 0.5814/0.2543, Z=4.5501 and Z-critical=1.96. The adjacent prose incorrectly calls these F statistics.
Range 1 and Range 5 inequalities are malformed as printed. The threshold table is omitted from the evidence card and no continuous binning rule is reconstructed.
2D counts 30/13/5/0/0 and 3D counts 17/10/15/3/3 were checked; retained only as qualitative evidence that 2D values concentrate in lower bins.

figures_checked.reason: General multiscale profile illustration; scale bands are background and do not provide paper-specific data.
2D stylus versus 3D laser acquisition difference is preserved textually with the finite-tip warning.
Ra definition is represented exactly by the verified equation and sign convention.
Sample-position template and workflow do not add reusable mechanism evidence beyond the selected-area caveat.
Instrument reading flowchart is procedural and omitted.
2D processing flowchart is summarized by cross-line fitting, node statistics and limitations.
The least-squares plane and Z-residual definition are unambiguously described in text and E1.
Minimal irreplaceable visual linking spatial location, quadtree ancestry and 5/2.5/1.25 mm roughness scales; crop retains all panels and the full caption.
Detailed signature/histogram is redundant with Fig. 8, the local-statistics description and reported example values.
Sparse 2D signature mainly illustrates missing unsampled cells; this is preserved textually as line undersampling.
Method-dependent bin distribution is represented by Table 4 aggregates and Table 6 counts.

omitted_sections: General mortar-adhesion background and standards survey

uncertain_items.detail: The method gives a 5 micrometer stylus tip radius and detector measuring force of 4 nM, while the Discussion calls the needle size 4 micrometers. The force unit is dimensionally suspect and no silent correction to mN was made.

A 10 mm by 10 mm area at 10 micrometer pitch would contain roughly one million regular grid points, not about 10,000 as reported. Neither value is used to set the solver mesh without raw-data confirmation.

The text introduces mean Ra values per line, but Table 1 is titled standard deviation from 2D Ra data. The individual table values are therefore not interpreted beyond their use in the authors' F-test.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.14, Table 4 and following paragraph
uncertain_items.detail: Table 4 and the method define a Z-test with Z=4.5501 and Z-critical=1.96; the prose immediately below writes F and F-critical. The evidence card follows the table/test definition and records the typo.
The first and last bins use inconsistent inequality layout; the bins are not retained as a reusable threshold rule.

uncertain_items.item: Reported analyzed area of 1.25 mm by 10 mm
uncertain_items.detail: This wording conflicts with the 10 mm by 10 mm 3D sample and 1.25 mm by 1.25 mm fourth-level node definitions. It was omitted from retained quantitative parameters.

known_warnings: The paper uses Ra for a 3D areal calculation; downstream implementation must define whether this is a source-specific Ra or an Sa-like metric.
The study reports average absolute height only and no PSD, correlation length, slope, curvature, directional spectrum or finite-spine accessibility.
The 2D-to-3D mean ratio is instrument-, sampling- and material-specific and must not be used as a universal conversion.
Sampling areas were qualitatively selected by one observer to avoid macro-scale imperfections, which can underrepresent service-surface defects.
Firing temperatures create sample groups but the paper does not establish a calibrated firing-temperature-to-topography relationship.
The paper contains no spine contact, friction, load, compliance or fracture evidence.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows.
